title: Terraform parsing failure
description: A parsing failure in Terraform indicates that the code cannot be interpreted by the Terraform engine. This may be due to syntax errors, invalid resource definitions, or unsupported constructs. Such failures prevent infrastructure from being provisioned or updated, and may hide configuration errors that could lead to security or operational issues.
message: Terraform code could not be parsed. Fix syntax or structural errors.
impact: Parsing failures block infrastructure deployment, can halt CI/CD pipelines, and may mask deeper configuration or security issues.
recommendation: Validate all Terraform files using 'terraform validate' before committing. Fix any syntax or structural errors reported by the tool. Use version control and code review to catch issues early.

compliant.description: All fields are valid and properly defined.

devsecops.security: Parsing failures may hide misconfigurations that could lead to insecure deployments.
devsecops.automation: Automated validation should be part of CI/CD to prevent broken code from reaching production.
devsecops.compliance: Ensures that only valid, reviewable code is deployed, supporting audit and compliance requirements.

why_is_this_an_issue: If Terraform code cannot be parsed, infrastructure changes cannot be applied, and errors may go unnoticed until deployment time, increasing risk.
how_to_fix: Run 'terraform validate' and correct all reported errors. Use editor plugins for syntax checking and enforce code review for all changes.

security_mappings.CERT: MSC00-J: Use comments judiciously and document assumptions
